claim: Certifies the pair-stabilized three-level shell gap exactly via (u-1)^2 Q(u)+v^2 R(u,v^2) with nonnegative integer coefficients.
claim_boundary: This proves the pair-stabilized 3-level family, not the full multilevel Barrier Schur Compression Lemma.

theorem.certificate: Under A=u+v, B=u-v, the polynomial becomes P_w=(u-1)^2 Q_w(u)+v^2 R_w(u,v^2), where Q_w and R_w have nonnegative integer coefficients.
theorem.consequence: P_w(A,B)>=0 for all A,B>=0. Equality occurs only on the equal diagonal in this pair-stabilized family.
theorem.name: Exact pair-stabilized shell gap certificate
theorem.normalization: P_w(A,B)=A_w(1)(A^2+B^2+22)^(w/2)-24^(w/2)(n11 AB+n10(A+B)+n00), where n11,n10,n00 are the pair membership profile counts.
theorem.role_in_global_proof: This closes the exact pair-stabilized 3-level obstruction and supplies a local model for the Barrier Schur Compression Lemma.
theorem.statement: For w=12,16, restrict to vectors with two distinguished coordinates A,B and the other 22 coordinates equal to C. By homogeneity set C=1. The shell gap is nonnegative for all A,B>=0.
